system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Conversation started: Sunday, May 03, 2026 03:07 PM
Model: MiniMax M2.7 Highspeed
Provider: minimax

You are on a text messaging communication platform, Telegram. Standard markdown is automatically converted to Telegram format. Supported: **bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~, ||spoiler||, `inline code`, ```code blocks```, [links](url), and ## headers. Telegram has NO table syntax — prefer bullet lists or labeled key: value pairs over pipe tables (any tables you do emit are auto-rewritten into row-group bullets, which you can produce directly for cleaner output). You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) appear as photos, audio (.ogg) sends as voice bubbles, and videos (.mp4) play inline. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as native photos.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"content": "     1|---
     2|name: hyperliquid-paper-trading
     3|description: Hyperliquid paper trading setup — wallet auth, testnet API, ccxt workaround
     4|---
     5|
     6|# Hyperliquid Paper Trading Setup
     7|
     8|## Setup
     9|
    10|```bash
    11|cd ~/.hermes/scripts
    12|~/.local/bin/uv venv hlexec --python 3.11
    13|~/.local/bin/uv pip install hyperliquid --python hlexec/bin/python
    14|```
    15|
    16|Helper script: `~/.hermes/scripts/hyperliquid_paper.py`
    17|
    18|## Authentication
    19|
    20|Hyperliquid uses **wallet-based auth**, NOT API key/secret.
    21|- `apiKey` and `walletAddress` = wallet address (0x...)
    22|- Private key NOT required for read operations
    23|- Private key only needed for trade execution
    24|
    25|```python
    26|import hyperliquid.ccxt as hl
    27|
    28|exchange = hl.hyperliquid()
    29|exchange.set_sandbox_mode(True)
    30|exchange.apiKey = '0xYOUR_WALLET_ADDRESS'
    31|exchange.walletAddress = '0xYOUR_WALLET_ADDRESS'
    32|```
    33|
    34|## Critical: ccxt Bug on Testnet
    35|
    36|`load_markets()` **crashes** on testnet — `fetch_spot_markets` fails when some tokens have `None` base names → `TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for +: 'NoneType' and 'str'`.
    37|
    38|**Workaround**: Use direct REST API requests.
    39|
    40|## Direct API (RELIABLE)
    41|
    42|```python
    43|import requests
    44|
    45|WALLET = "0xYOUR_WALLET_ADDRESS"
    46|BASE_URL = "https://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz"
    47|
    48|def info(payload: dict) -> dict:
    49|    return requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/info", json=payload, timeout=10).json()
    50|
    51|# Balance
    52|info({"type": "clearinghouseState", "user": WALLET})
    53|
    54|# Prices (all perpetuals)
    55|data = info({"type": "metaAndAssetCtxs"})
    56|# data[0]['universe'] = asset list, data[1] = price contexts
    57|```
    58|
    59|## What Works via ccxt
    60|
    61|- `fetch_balance()` ✅
    62|- `create_market_buy_order()` ❌ (crashes on load_markets)
    63|- `fetch_open_orders()` ❌ (crashes on load_markets)
    64|
    65|## Funding Testnet
    66|
    67|Go to https://app.hyperliquid.xyz/testnet → connect wallet → faucet/transfer USDC.
    68|", "total_lines": 67, "file_size": 1786, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|---
     2|name: crypto-paper-trader
     3|description: Build a local crypto paper trader using Binance public API for live strategy validation — no exchange API keys needed. Useful when Alpaca/WunderTrading demo accounts don't support spot crypto.
     4|---
     5|
     6|# Crypto Paper Trader — Live Strategy Validator
     7|
     8|## Trigger
     9|Use when the user wants to paper trade or live-test a crypto trading strategy but doesn't have API keys for an exchange, or the available paper trading accounts (Alpaca, WunderTrading demo) don't support the desired asset or spot trading.
    10|
    11|## Approach: Binance Public API + Local Simulation
    12|
    13|Binance provides OHLCV data via public REST endpoint — no API key needed for reading prices. This enables a fully functional paper trader with zero credentials.
    14|
    15|### Step 1 — Verify Binance Public Data Access
    16|```bash
    17|curl -s "https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=4h&limit=10"
    18|```
    19|If this returns candlestick data, you're good to go.
    20|
    21|### Step 2 — Build the Paper Trader
    22|Core components:
    23|1. **Data fetch** — `requests.get()` to `https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines` with symbol, interval, limit
    24|2. **Indicators** — pandas/TA library (RSI, EMA, ATR, Bollinger, etc.)
    25|3. **Signal detection** — boolean masks for entry conditions
    26|4. **Position simulation** — walk-forward loop through subsequent candles simulating TP/SL/trailing
    27|5. **State persistence** — JSON file for capital, open trades, trade history
    28|6. **Discord webhook** — notify on entry/exit (optional, via `requests.post`)
    29|
    30|### Step 3 — Key Binance Endpoints (No Auth)
    31|| Purpose | URL |
    32||---------|-----|
    33|| Klines/candles | `https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=4h&limit=200` |
    34|| 24hr ticker | `https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ticker/24hr?symbol=BTCUSDT` |
    35|| Order book | `https://api.binance.com/api/v3/depth?symbol=BTCUSDT&limit=10` |
    36|
    37|**Timeframe mapping:** `1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d, 1w`
    38|
    39|### Step 4 — Limitations of This Approach
    40|- **No real orders executed** — prices are real but orders are simulated
    41|- Cannot verify fill quality or slippage reality
    42|- Good for: strategy validation, signal timing, expectancy confirmation
    43|- Not a substitute for real exchange paper trading when available
    44|
    45|## Common Blockers Encountered
    46|| Platform | Blocker |
    47||----------|---------|
    48|| Alpaca | Paper trading does NOT support crypto — only US equities/ETFs |
    49|| WunderTrading | Demo accounts are futures-only, not spot |
    50|| Binance Testnet | Requires separate testnet API keys from testnet.binance.vision |
    51|| TradingView | Paper trading only for stocks/forex, not crypto |
    52|
    53|## ⚠️ CRITICAL — Marco's Paper Trading System (UNIFIED as of May 3)
    54|
    55|**UPDATE May 3:** `paper_trader.py` was modified to call WunderTrading API directly. The local simulation and real order execution are now UNIFIED into a single system.
    56|
    57|### Unified System
    58|- Script: `/home/Obsidian-Vault/Trading/paper_trader.py`
    59|  - Contains: `WunderTradingAPI` class, `wt_place_order()`, `wt_close_strategy()`, `sync_wt_positions()`
    60|  - Local strategies execute real orders on WunderTrading DemoAccount
    61|- Log: `/home/Obsidian-Vault/Trading/paper_trader.log`
    62|- Cron: "Paper Trading Updates" every 30 min → `~/.hermes/scripts/paper_trader_status.sh`
    63|- Run: `cd /home/Obsidian-Vault/Trading && python3 paper_trader.py`
    64|- State: `wun_states_v3/*.json` (includes `wt_strategy_id` after sync)
    65|
    66|### Current Real Positions (WunderTrading DemoAccount)
    67|| Pair | Strategy | Side | Entry | Leverage |
    68||------|----------|------|-------|----------|
    69|| CRVUSDT | OBV_LONG_BTC_2h | 🟢 LONG | $0.2251 | 1x |
    70|| BNBUSDT | BB_REV_LONG_BNB_1h | 🔴 SHORT | $622.19 | 1x |
    71|| XRPUSDT | STOCH_BOTH_XRP_12h | 🟢 LONG | $1.3791 | 5x |
    72|
    73|### Check Status
    74|```bash
    75|# Direct API check
    76|python3 /home/marco/.hermes/skills/productivity/wundertrading-api-direct/scripts/check_positions.py
    77|
    78|# Via paper_trader log
    79|tail -50 /home/Obsidian-Vault/Trading/paper_trader.log
    80|
    81|# Verify single process
    82|ps aux | grep paper_trader | grep -v grep
    83|```
    84|
    85|## Files Created
    86|- `/home/Obsidian-Vault/Trading/paper_trader.py` — RSI Trend 40-60 live trader
    87|- Strategy: BTC/USDT 4H, RSI 40-60, EMA21>EMA50, SL 3× ATR, 3-tranche TP + trailing
    88|
    89|## Example Discord Notify Pattern
    90|```python
    91|def notify(msg: str, color: int = 0x00FF00):
    92|    url = os.environ.get("DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL")
    93|    if not url: return
    94|    requests.post(url, json={
    95|        "embeds": [{
    96|            "title": "Paper Trader Alert",
    97|            "description": msg,

name: binance-trading
description: Binance spot and futures trading via python-requests — API setup, order types, WebSocket streaming, and common pitfalls. No official SDK required.
---

# Binance Trading Skill

## Trigger
Use when the user wants to trade on Binance via Python script (no official SDK), need spot/futures data, or want to set up automated trading on Binance.

## Authentication

### API Key Setup
1. Go to [Binance API Management](https://www.binance.com/en/my/settings/api-management)
2. Create a new API key (System-generated or manually entered)
3. For futures: enable "Enable Futures" permission
4. Store keys securely — never hardcode

```python
import os
import requests

API_KEY = os.environ["BINANCE_API_KEY"]
API_SECRET = os.environ["BINANCE_API_SECRET"]
```

## Spot vs Futures Endpoints

### Spot (No Auth for Public Data)
| Purpose | URL |
|---------|-----|
| Test connectivity | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ping` |
| Exchange info | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/exchangeInfo` |
| Klines | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=1h&limit=500` |
| 24hr ticker | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/ticker/24hr?symbol=BTCUSDT` |
| Order book | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/depth?symbol=BTCUSDT&limit=100` |
| My trades | `GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/myTrades?symbol=BTCUSDT` |
| Place order (SIGNED) | `POST https://api.binance.com/api/v3/order` |

### Spot Authenticated Requests
```python
import time
import hashlib
import requests

def binance_spot_request(method: str, endpoint: str, params: dict = None):
    """Sign and send authenticated Binance Spot API request."""
    params = params or {}
    params["timestamp"] = int(time.time() * 1000)
    params["recvWindow"] = 5000

    # Build query string and sign with HMAC SHA256
    query = "&".join([f"{k}={v}" for k, v in params.items()])
    signature = hashlib.sha256((API_SECRET + query).encode()).hexdigest()
    params["signature"] = signature

    headers = {"X-MBX-APIKEY": API_KEY}
    url = f"https://api.binance.com{endpoint}"

    if method == "GET":
        return requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    elif method == "POST":
        return requests.post(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    elif method == "DELETE":
        return requests.delete(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
```

### Futures API Endpoints
| Purpose | URL |
|---------|-----|
| Futures exchange info | `GET https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/exchangeInfo` |
| Klines | `GET https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=1h&limit=500` |
| Account info (SIGNED) | `GET https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v2/account` |
| Place order (SIGNED) | `POST https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/order` |
| Position mode (SIGNED) | `POST https://fapi.binance.com/fapi/v1/positionSide/dual` |

### Futures Signed Request
```python
import hmac
import time

def binance_futures_request(method: str, endpoint: str, params: dict = None):
    """Sign and send authenticated Binance Futures API request."""
    params = params or {}
    params["timestamp"] = int(time.time() * 1000)
    params["recvWindow"] = 5000

    query = "&".join([f"{k}={v}" for k, v in params.items()])
    signature = hmac.new(API_SECRET.encode(), query.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
    params["signature"] = signature

    headers = {"X-MBX-APIKEY": API_KEY}
    base = "https://fapi.binance.com"
    url = f"{base}{endpoint}"

    if method == "GET":
        return requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    elif method == "POST":
        return requests.post(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
    elif method == "DELETE":
        return requests.delete(url, headers=headers, params=params, timeout=10).json()
```

## Order Types

### Market Order
```python
# Spot
order = binance_spot_request("POST", "/api/v3/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "side": "BUY",
    "type": "MARKET",
    "quantity": "0.001"
})

# Futures
order = binance_futures_request("POST", "/fapi/v1/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "side": "BUY",
    "type": "MARKET",
    "quantity": "0.001"
})
```

### Limit Order
```python
# Spot
order = binance_spot_request("POST", "/api/v3/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "side": "BUY",
    "type": "LIMIT",
    "quantity": "0.001",
    "price": "50000",       # Limit price
    "timeInForce": "GTC"    # Good-Til-Cancelled
})

# Futures
order = binance_futures_request("POST", "/fapi/v1/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "side": "BUY",
    "type": "LIMIT",
    "quantity": "0.001",
    "price": "50000",
    "timeInForce": "GTC"
})
```

### Stop-Limit Order
```python
# Spot — sell if price drops below stop
order = binance_spot_request("POST", "/api/v3/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "side": "SELL",
    "type": "STOP_LOSS_LIMIT",
    "quantity": "0.001",
    "stopPrice": "49000",
    "price": "48900",
    "timeInForce": "GTC"
})
```

### Futures: Stop-Market / Take-Profit-Market
```python
# Futures — conditional market order on stop
order = binance_futures_request("POST", "/fapi/v1/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "side": "SELL",
    "type": "STOP_MARKET",
    "quantity": "0.001",
    "stopPrice": "48000",
    "reduceOnly": True
})
```

### Query Order
```python
# Spot — check order status
result = binance_spot_request("GET", "/api/v3/order", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "orderId": order["orderId"]
})
```

## WebSocket Streaming

### Single Stream (Klines)
```python
import websocket
import json

SYMBOL = "btcusdt"
STREAM = f"wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/{SYMBOL}@kline_1h"

def on_message(ws, message):
    data = json.loads(message)
    kline = data["k"]
    print(f"OHLC: {kline['o']}, {kline['h']}, {kline['l']}, {kline['c']}")

ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(STREAM, on_message=on_message)
ws.run_forever()
```

### Combined Streams
```python
# Multiple streams combined
STREAMS = [
    "btcusdt@kline_1h",
    "btcusdt@depth20@100ms",
    "btcusdt@ticker"
]
STREAM_URL = f"wss://stream.binance.com:9443/stream?streams={'/'.join(STREAMS)}"

def on_message(ws, message):
    data = json.loads(message)["data"]
    stream = data["stream"]
    if "kline" in stream:
        print(f"KLINE: {data['data']['k']['c']}")
    elif "ticker" in stream:
        print(f"TICKER: {data['data']['c']}")
```

### User Data Stream (Spot) — Long-lived account updates
```python
# Step 1: Get listen key
r = binance_spot_request("POST", "/api/v3/userDataStream")
LISTEN_KEY = r["listenKey"]

# Step 2: Keepalive (every 30 min)
requests.put(f"https://api.binance.com/api/v3/userDataStream?listenKey={LISTEN_KEY}",
             headers={"X-MBX-APIKEY": API_KEY}, timeout=10)

# Step 3: Listen
ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(
    f"wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/{LISTEN_KEY}",
    on_message=lambda ws, msg: print(json.loads(msg))
)
ws.run_forever()
```

## Binance Futures: Position Mode Gotcha

**⚠️ CRITICAL:** By default, Binance Futures uses **One-way mode** (position mode). If you need to hedge (hold both long and short simultaneously), you must enable **Hedge Mode**.

```python
# Enable Hedge Mode (positionSide must be LONG or SHORT on every order)
binance_futures_request("POST", "/fapi/v1/positionSide/dual", {
    "dualLongPositionSide": True,
    "dualShortPositionSide": True,
    "positionSide": "BOTH"  # or "LONG" or "SHORT"
})
```

**Note:** `positionSide` must be explicitly set on every order when using hedge mode.

## Common Pitfalls

| Pitfall | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| 400 — Invalid signature | Clock skew > 5s | Sync system time: `sudo ntpdate pool.ntp.org` |
| 400 — Filter failure | Price/quantity below min notional | Check `exchangeInfo` for min values |
| 400 — Invalid symbol | Wrong pair format | Use "BTCUSDT" not "BTC/USDT" |
| 401 — Unauthorized | Wrong API key or IP not whitelisted | Check Binance settings |
| 429 — Rate limit | Too many requests | Check `X-MBX-USE_WEIGHT` header; use rate limiter |
| 1015 — Too many new orders | Spot: > 5 orders/s | Use `ORDER_COUNT_LIMIT`; Futures: 10/10s |
| `timestamp` out of window | System clock drift | Ensure recvWindow=5000 and clock synced |

## Rate Limits

- Spot: **1200 requests/minute** (weighted by usage)
- Spot Orders: **5 orders/second** (for same symbol)
- Futures: **2400 requests/minute**
- Futures Orders: **10 orders/10 seconds** per symbol
- WebSocket: **5 messages/second** outbound

## Symbol Name Convention

- Spot: `BTCUSDT`, `ETHUSDT`, `BNBUSDT`
- Futures: `BTCUSDT`, `ETHUSDT` (same naming)
- Min quantity: check via `exchangeInfo` — some coins have 3 decimal precision, others 8

## Useful Scripts Location

Scripts for this skill live in: `~/.hermes/scripts/binance/`
"}

name: bybit-hyperliquid-trading
description: Bybit spot/perpetual and Hyperliquid trading setup — API auth, REST/WS, ccxt integration, and pitfalls for both exchanges.
---

# Bybit & Hyperliquid Trading Skill

## When to Use
Use when the user wants to trade on **Bybit** (inverse/linear perpetual, spot) or **Hyperliquid** (perpetuals, spot, L2 order book), via Python script with or without official SDKs.

---

# Bybit Trading

## API Setup

1. Go to [Bybit API](https://www.bybit.com/user/en setting/APITrading)
2. Create API key — select permissions: "Read-Only", "Trade", "Withdraw" as needed
3. Set IP whitelist (or leave open for development)
4. Store `api_key` and `api_secret` — never hardcode

```python
import os
API_KEY = os.environ["BYBIT_API_KEY"]
API_SECRET = os.environ["BYBIT_API_SECRET"]
```

## REST API Base URLs

| Environment | URL |
|-------------|-----|
| Spot (HTTPS) | `https://api.bybit.com` |
| Spot Testnet | `https://api-testnet.bybit.com` |
| Perpetual (HTTPS) | `https://api.bybit.com` |
| Perpetual Testnet | `https://api-testnet.bybit.com` |

## Bybit Perpetual (Linear) — Signed Request

```python
import time
import hashlib
import hmac
import requests

BASE_URL = "https://api.bybit.com"

def bybit_request(method: str, endpoint: str, params: dict = None, recv_window: int = 5000):
    """Sign and send authenticated Bybit Perpetual API request."""
    params = params or {}
    params["api_key"] = API_KEY
    params["recv_window"] = recv_window
    params["timestamp"] = str(int(time.time() * 1000))

    # Build sorted query string
    sorted_params = sorted(params.items())
    query_string = "&".join([f"{k}={v}" for k, v in sorted_params])

    # HMAC SHA256 sign
    signature = hmac.new(
        API_SECRET.encode(),
        query_string.encode(),
        hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()

    params["sign"] = signature

    if method == "GET":
        return requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}{endpoint}", params=params, timeout=10).json()
    elif method == "POST":
        return requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}{endpoint}", data=params, timeout=10).json()
```

## Bybit Key Endpoints

### Public (No Auth)
```python
# Get instrument info
requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/v2/public/tickers", params={"symbol": "BTCUSD"}, timeout=10)

# Klines
requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/v2/public/kline/list", params={
    "symbol": "BTCUSD",
    "interval": "60",      # 1, 3, 5, 15, 30, 60, 120, 240, 360, 720, D, W, M
    "limit": 200
}, timeout=10).json()

# Order book (Spot uses /v3/public, Perp uses /v2/public)
requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/v2/public/orderBook/L2", params={
    "symbol": "BTCUSD",
    "price": 0.5  # optional: depth tick
}, timeout=10).json()
```

### Authenticated
```python
# Get positions (linear perpetual)
positions = bybit_request("GET", "/v2/private/position/list", {
    "symbol": "BTCUSD"
})

# Get wallet balance
balance = bybit_request("GET", "/v2/private/wallet/balance", {
    "coin": "USDT"
})

# Place order
order = bybit_request("POST", "/v2/private/order/create", {
    "side": "Buy",
    "symbol": "BTCUSD",
    "order_type": "Market",
    "qty": "1",           # In BTC for inverse, USDT for linear
    "price": "50000",     # Required for limit orders
    "time_in_force": "GoodTillCancel"
})
```

## Bybit Order Parameters

| Parameter | Values | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| `side` | Buy/Sell | |
| `order_type` | Market/Limit | |
| `qty` | Number | BTC: 1 = 1 BTC, USDT: 1 = 1 USDT notional |
| `price` | Number | Required for Limit orders |
| `time_in_force` | GoodTillCancel/ImmediateOrCancel/FillOrKill | |
| `reduce_only` | true/false | For perpetual |
| `close_on_trigger` | true/false | Close existing position |
| `position_idx` | 0/1/2 | 0=One-way, 1=Buy hedge, 2=Sell hedge |

## Bybit WebSocket (Spot & Perp)

```python
import websocket
import json
import hmac
import base64
import time

# For Spot: wss://stream.bybit.com:443/v3/public/{category}
# For Perp: wss://stream.bybit.com:443/v5/public/linear

SYMBOL = "BTCUSD"
WS_URL = f"wss://stream.bybit.com:443/v2/public"

def on_open(ws):
    # Subscribe to orderbook and trades
    ws.send(json.dumps({
        "op": "subscribe",
        "args": [f"orderBook.{SYMBOL}", f"trade.{SYMBOL}"]
    }))

def on_message(ws, message):
    data = json.loads(message)
    print(data)

ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(WS_URL, on_message=on_message, on_open=on_open)
ws.run_forever()
```

### Private WebSocket (Perp) — Auth
```python
AUTH_URL = "wss://stream.bybit.com:443/v2/private"

def generate_signature(api_secret, message):
    return hmac.new(api_secret.encode(), message.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()

expires = int(time.time() * 1000) + 10000
msg = f"GET/realtime{expires}"
signature = generate_signature(API_SECRET, msg)

auth_payload = {
    "op": "auth",
    "args": [API_KEY, expires, signature]
}

def on_open(ws):
    ws.send(json.dumps(auth_payload))
    ws.send(json.dumps({"op": "subscribe", "args": ["position"]}))
```

---

# Hyperliquid Trading

## API Setup

```bash
pip install hyperliquid
```

Hyperliquid uses **wallet-based auth** (not API key/secret).
- `walletAddress` = your 0x... Ethereum address
- Private key only needed for signing trade requests
- For read-only: only `walletAddress` required

```python
import hyperliquid.ccxt as hl

exchange = hl.hyperliquid()
exchange.set_sandbox_mode(True)  # Use testnet
exchange.walletAddress = "0xYOUR_WALLET_ADDRESS"
```

## Testnet vs Mainnet

| Environment | URL |
|-------------|-----|
| Testnet | `https://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz` |
| Mainnet | `https://api.hyperliquid.xyz` |

## ⚠️ CRITICAL — ccxt `load_markets()` Bug on Testnet

`fetch_spot_markets()` crashes on testnet when some tokens have `None` base names:
```
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for +: 'NoneType' and 'str'
```

**Fix:** Use direct REST API for all operations.

## Direct REST API (RELIABLE)

```python
import requests

WALLET = "0xYOUR_WALLET_ADDRESS"
BASE_URL = "https://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz"

def info(payload: dict) -> dict:
    return requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/info", json=payload, timeout=10).json()

# Meta + asset contexts (prices, circulating supply, etc.)
data = info({"type": "metaAndAssetCtxs"})
# data[0]['universe'] = list of assets
# data[1] = price/market contexts per asset

# User balance/positions
info({"type": "clearinghouseState", "user": WALLET})

# Open orders (requires wallet address set)
info({"type": "openOrders", "user": WALLET})

# Tradeable assets
info({"type": "metaAndAssetCtxs"})
info({"type": "allMids"})  # All mid prices
```

## Place Order via REST

```python
import json
from web3 import Web3

# For trading, use the exchange endpoint (not info)
# Docs: https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/python-sdk

# Use hyperliquid SDK's sign_and_send
# See: https://github.com/hyperliquid-perpetual/hyperliquid-python-sdk

from hyperliquid.exchange import Exchange
from hyperliquid.utils import sign_sets_perp_open
from hyperliquid.api import API

# Setup
api = API()
exchange = Exchange(api, WALLET)

# Place order
# order = exchange.order(...)
```

## Hyperliquid WebSocket

```python
import websocket
import json

WS_URL = "wss://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz/ws"

def on_open(ws):
    # Subscribe to trades and orderbook
    ws.send(json.dumps({
        "method": "subscribe",
        "subscription": {"type": "trades", "coin": "BTC"}
    }))
    ws.send(json.dumps({
        "method": "subscribe",
        "subscription": {"type": "l2Book", "coin": "BTC"}
    }))

def on_message(ws, message):
    print(json.loads(message))

ws = websocket.WebSocketApp(WS_URL, on_message=on_message, on_open=on_open)
ws.run_forever()
```

## Hyperliquid: What Works / What Doesn't

| Operation | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| `fetch_balance()` | ✅ | Via ccxt or direct REST |
| `fetch_ticker()` | ✅ | Via ccxt |
| `create_market_order()` | ✅ | Via ccxt on mainnet |
| `create_limit_order()` | ✅ | Via ccxt |
| `fetch_open_orders()` | ✅ | Via ccxt on mainnet |
| `load_markets()` testnet | ❌ | ccxt crash — use direct REST |
| `fetch_spot_markets()` | ❌ | ccxt crash on testnet |
| Open/close perpetual | ✅ | Via SDK or direct REST |

## Funding Testnet

1. Go to https://app.hyperliquid.xyz/testnet
2. Connect wallet (MetaMask/WalletConnect)
3. Use faucet to get test USDC

## Common Pitfalls

### Bybit
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| 10003 — Verify failed | Signature mismatch | Ensure params sorted alphabetically, timestamp correct |
| 10004 — Request expired | recv_window too small | Increase to 10000 or sync clock |
| 10005 — Permission denied | API key lacks Trade permission | Re-create key with Trade enabled |
| 10001 — Order price out of range | Price precision wrong | Round to tick size from instrument info |
| 10014 — Duplicate order | `order_link_id` already used | Use unique ID or omit for auto-generated |

### Hyperliquid
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| ccxt `load_markets()` crash | None base name in response | Use direct REST instead |
| 400 on order | Wallet not connected to exchange | Fund testnet account first |
| Stale price data | WebSocket reconnect needed | Implement heartbeat/reconnect logic |

## Quick Reference

- **Bybit Perp** — Linear (USDT-margined): `/v2/private/...` endpoints
- **Bybit Perp** — Inverse (BTC-margined): `/v2/public/...` for reads, `/v2/private/...` for writes
- **Hyperliquid** — All perpetuals, mainnet + testnet. Uses `0x` address as identity.
- **Both** — Support sandbox/testnet modes — always test before going live

## Scripts Location

Scripts for this skill live in: `~/.hermes/scripts/bybit-hyperliquid/`
"}

- **Order types** — Market, Limit, Stop-Loss, Stop-Market for both spot and futures
- **WebSocket streaming** — Single/combined streams for klines, orderbook, ticker, user data

- **Pitfalls table** — Covers signature errors (clock skew), filter failures, rate limits, IP whitelist issues
- **Rate limits** — Spot 1200/min, Futures 2400/min, order count limits

### 2. `bybit-hyperliquid-trading/SKILL.md` (9,721 bytes)

- **Hyperliquid** — Wallet-based auth, direct REST API workaround for ccxt testnet crash (`load_markets()` bug), WebSocket subscriptions
- **⚠️ Critical bug** — Hyperliquid ccxt `fetch_spot_markets()` crashes on testnet due to `None` base names — resolved via direct REST
